the moment makes a lot more sense if you've also seen spinal tap. i felt the same way you did after watching the moment, but i watched spinal tap the following night on a whim & i started to see what charli was trying to get at better. spinal tap is a mockumentary from the 80s about a fictional british rock band touring through the us at the end of their prime. it is foundational to the mockumentary genre, & charli has said in interviews & promo that there would be no moment w/o spinal tap. i think the movies have the same goals but spinal tap achieves them much better & more completely, including the goals like stakes & humor that you mentioned. the moment is definitely trying to be a modern spinal tap, so after watching both i could see the moment's train of thought & goals better. ofc the moment is a stand alone piece of work & my opinion on it didnt change after seeing spinal tap, but i suggest watching spinal tap as a companion piece of sorts, especially if you felt disappointed by the moment. its also just a good movie lol
Those flashes have a death count

I love your thoughts, I honestly loved the movie, everything from the flashing “glitches” saying things like 0 elasticity (her insecurities manifesting around her) to the ending monologue where she finally admits that she doesn’t like herself, famous or not, popular or not. I feel like the movie wasn’t so much learning how to let go necessarily of a moment or an event and more like a self catharsis of her at least acknowledging her faults, her insecurities and the way she lets it run her life and relationships, and the movie ends with bitter sweet symphony , which isn’t saying everything is alright now, but I feel like it’s saying that she’s at least in a better place than the start of the film and she can now take the next step in that path of self acceptance

Even though I haven't seen the movie myself, from your narration I think one change could've made it far more interesting than it already is: If they showed that the concert film in the movie actually becomes a big hit and everybody around her is hyping her up and families are watching it but charli feels unsatisfied and she does something in public to piss people off about her and the movie and that combined with the bank related scandal take a big toll on her fame and brat, in the meantime, dies: for which charli is happy cause deep inside all along she wished to be liberated of it. Taking this approach in my opinion would have also led to a large part of her fanbase actually slowly shift towards moving on from brat, and would've urged people to listen more to wuthering heights because subconsciously people would connect the charli there and irl and take the message that she at last wants us to move on from brat. Just smth i conceptualised :3

I really liked it! I think the bank collapsing was more of a social pressure rather than a monetary one, with charli being stressed because people are starting to turn on her and brat as a whole. I think she was starting to feel anxiety about how to follow up brat and let her label (and Kylie) pressure her into keeping it going and expanding the fanbase. Once the bank collapses it sends her into a mania where she can’t face the world and finally concludes that accepting the end of brat was the best way forward (I also think it was a very funny decision to show that the only way to kill brat was to let the people in the industry have their way). Overall, my biggest gripe with the pacing is that the ending felt rushed. I think we could have used slightly less if the Ibiza trip and slightly more of the tension been charli and the label leading to the downfall at the end. Still thought it was really fun and agree with you that I love the way it was shot and acted.
